purpose: Durable reference timing and audio-fidelity library for Galaga-aligned polish work in Aurora and future fixed-screen arcade packs.
rule: Use measured reference artifacts first for timing, motion, audio, pacing, and transition decisions. Manual listening/viewing is secondary validation.

eventFamilies.label: Opening Sequence
eventFamilies.focus: Full start phrase, arrival signal, first visible alien arrivals, first convoy pulse.
notes: Preserve the full canonical game-start phrase where feasible.
Widen the game window before shortening the phrase.
This family should be the default reference for shell-to-play handoff decisions.

eventFamilies.label: Formation Arrival And Convoy Cadence
eventFamilies.focus: Arrival pacing, convoy cadence, first dive timing, and the relationship between movement and recurring board audio.
notes: This is one of the main timing-sensitive areas for subjective “feel”.
Use measured board motion from the archived gameplay video before nudging cadence by ear.

eventFamilies.label: Enemy Dive And Charge
eventFamilies.focus: Dive-start timing versus visible enemy commitment and fall path.
notes: The charge cue should line up with the moment an alien visibly commits to the dive, not with bullets or later descent frames.

eventFamilies.label: Capture Beam And Capture Flow
eventFamilies.focus: Beam deploy, successful capture, retreat with carried ship, destruction of captured ship, and rescue/join.
notes: This family is already structurally split in Aurora and is a strong candidate for deeper timing probes later.
The same comparison pattern will transfer well to Galaxian-style special-state flows in future games.

eventFamilies.label: Ship Loss And Respawn
eventFamilies.focus: Immediate death cue, visual loss sync, last-ship tension, and return-to-control pacing.
notes: Ordinary ship loss and terminal game-over ambience should remain separate concepts.
Future work should add more visual-timing measurements here, not just cue timestamps.

eventFamilies.label: Stage Clear And Next Stage
eventFamilies.focus: Stage clear spacing, next-stage announcement, spawn handoff, and transition quiet windows.
notes: This family should capture how much quiet space the player gets between stage completion and the next arrival.

eventFamilies.label: Challenge Entry, Results, And Perfect
eventFamilies.focus: Challenge announcement timing, setup handoff, results timing, perfect timing, and next-stage recovery.
notes: This is still one of the messiest live-feel families and should remain a primary tuning target.
Use the overlap budgets here before manual trimming or cue shortening.

eventFamilies.label: Boss Hit And Boss Death
eventFamilies.focus: Boss damage identity, boss death audibility, and visual emphasis at destruction.
notes: Boss destruction should read as a meaningful event both visually and audibly, not just a slightly larger ordinary kill.
A dedicated boss timing probe is still worth adding.

eventFamilies.label: High Score And Results Flow
eventFamilies.focus: Results-to-name-entry handoff and first-place versus non-first placement distinction.
notes: This family is already structurally represented in Aurora and should be easy to extend with richer timing checks later.

forAurora: Use the library before changing cue timing, clip length, movement pacing, or transition windows.
When a new polish issue appears, map it into one event family and add measured evidence there first.
Prefer updating timing windows and arrival delays before cutting canonical phrases.
forFutureGames: Copy the same structure for Galaxian or later games: canonical event families, measured reference artifacts, current runtime metrics, and comparison deltas.
Keep game-specific timing truth inside the game pack and use Platinum only for generic hooks and harness support.
